verses.text: A man among the tombs, possessed, none could bind, crying and cutting himself. Utter misery. Sin's bondage leaves a man wretched and wild.

He runs and worships Jesus; the spirit says, 'My name is Legion: for we are many.' Many tormentors. A host of evil had taken one soul captive.

The devils beg to enter the swine, which rush into the sea and drown. Destroyers. Evil ruins whatever it possesses.
The devils beg to enter the swine, which rush into the sea and drown. Destroyers. Evil ruins whatever it possesses.

The man is found 'clothed, and in his right mind'; the people, afraid, beg Jesus to depart. Strange fear. They prize their swine above the Saviour.

The healed man would follow; 'go home... and tell... how great things the Lord hath done.' A witness sent. Restored, he becomes a herald of mercy.

Jairus, a ruler, falls at His feet for his dying daughter; Jesus goes with him. Humble plea. Need brings even a ruler low before Christ.

A woman with a twelve-year issue touches His garment, 'If I may touch but his clothes,' and is healed. Trembling faith. The lightest touch of faith draws His power.

A woman with a twelve-year issue touches His garment, 'If I may touch but his clothes,' and is healed. Trembling faith. The lightest touch of faith draws His power.
'Who touched me?' the woman, fearing, falls down; 'Daughter, thy faith hath made thee whole; go in peace.' Faith owned. He will not let her healing go unblessed.

Word comes the child is dead; 'Be not afraid, only believe'; He takes Peter, James, and John. Faith in death. He bids the father trust Him still.

Amid the weeping He says, 'the damsel is not dead, but sleepeth'; they laugh; He puts them out. Scorned word. Unbelief mocks the very hope it needs.

Amid the weeping He says, 'the damsel is not dead, but sleepeth'; they laugh; He puts them out. Scorned word. Unbelief mocks the very hope it needs.
He takes her hand, 'Talitha cumi... Damsel... arise'; she rises and walks. Death undone. At His touch the grave gives up its child.
